## Section 2 - Point of view

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `pov.eyebrow` | Eyebrow | Point of view |
| `pov.kicker` | Kicker | Marketing isn't just the last mile. |
| `pov.headline` | Headline (uppercase on site) | If you wait until it's ready, you're already too late. |
| `pov.body.01` | Body | The worst time to figure out your market story is when you urgently need it. Yet many engineers treat marketing as something to bolt on at the very end, when the product is solid and the technology is ready. In hard tech, this costs time and runway. |
| `pov.link` | Link (end of section) | Read the essay → https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mXTznBJsOwonMlEeezdZjcLEiznj_ob5lOBihiWzF_Y/edit?usp=sharing |

### POV cards

| LOCATION | Card title | Card body |
|----------|------------|-----------|
| `pov.card.01` | Extending the runway | A credible investor narrative helps you raise faster, on better terms. |
| `pov.card.02` | Attracting talent | Mission and story are central to recruiting, hiring, and compensation. |
| `pov.card.03` | Finding partners | A strategic partner narrative opens early doors that cold outreach can't. |
| `pov.card.04` | Building social proof | Case studies and proof points can take years to build. Start early. |
| `pov.card.05` | Entering new verticals | Your second market needs positioning before you've finished the first. |
| `pov.card.06` | Crafting the narratives | Adjacent industries, regulators, acquirers - each needs their own story. |

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `pov.body.02` | Body (after cards) | When development cycles last 5-10 years and capital requirements run into the tens of millions, your market story cannot be just a launch asset. It should be a design input - one that shapes what you build, who you hire, and whether money shows up in time. |

---

## Section 3 - Two kinds of work

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `two-kinds.headline` | Headline | There are two kinds of marketing (both matter). |
| `two-kinds.table.col.foundational` | Table header | Foundational marketing |
| `two-kinds.table.col.episodic` | Table header | Episodic marketing |
| `two-kinds.table.foundational` | Table cell | The first is **foundational** - it happens early and produces clarity, not campaigns. Why should this company exist? Who is the user, the buyer, and the economic customer? What are you displacing, and what will a rational buyer need to say yes? These are the load-bearing elements of your commercial architecture. |
| `two-kinds.table.episodic` | Table cell | The second is **episodic** - triggered by specific moments in time: a fundraising or technical milestone, a key hire, a market expansion, a sudden shift in the competitive environment. These moments arrive faster than expected. The companies that handle them well don't scramble because they built the foundation. |
| `two-kinds.closing` | Body (centered below table) | You need someone to build the commercial architecture early and then show up ready at the moments that matter. |

### Case study: Teradar

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `work.teradar.tag` | Tag | Hard tech · Sensors |
| `work.teradar.title` | Title | Teradar |
| `work.teradar.body` | Body | Teradar had pre-commercial terahertz sensing technology and silicon expertise and needed to close a seed round. I worked with the CEO on their initial pitch deck, technical appendix, and investor narrative, and coached messaging through 60+ investor meetings. The $2M+ seed closed oversubscribed. The same foundation supported Series A, a $150M Series B, and later engagements including technical product videos and CES launch assets. |
| `work.teradar.quote` | Quote | (omit until ready) |
| `work.teradar.pill.01` | Pill | Investor narrative |
| `work.teradar.pill.02` | Pill | Technical marketing |
| `work.teradar.image.01` | Carousel image | img/work-teradar-techcrunch-feature.png |
| `work.teradar.image.02` | Carousel image | img/work-teradar-feature.png |

### Case study: Lux Labs

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `work.lux.tag` | Tag | Hard tech · Materials science |
| `work.lux.title` | Title | Lux Labs |
| `work.lux.body` | Body | Lux Labs was commercializing a transparent display film - they had proven MIT technology, significant website traffic, and a few early customers. But a 30-SKU catalog and spec-heavy product pages blocked conversion. I reduced the catalog to two SKUs, rebuilt pricing, and mapped e-commerce flows to customer use cases instead of materials specs and technical data sheets. Project fees paid back in under two months from increased gross margins. The pricing and packaging structure remained in place through acquisition. |
| `work.lux.quote` | Quote | Lux Labs hired Jeremy to support our ClearBright product launch. He helped us overhaul our e-commerce approach by redesigning our website, simplifying our pricing and packaging, and creating new marketing content. He helped us step back and evaluate how to communicate with our customers. |
| `work.lux.quote.meta` | Quote meta | Spencer Powers, CEO, Lux Labs |
| `work.lux.pill.01` | Pill | Go-to-market |
| `work.lux.pill.02` | Pill | Pricing strategy |
| `work.lux.image.01` | Carousel image | img/work-lux-feature-02.png |
| `work.lux.image.02` | Carousel image | img/work-lux-feature-01.png |

### Case study: Reframe Systems

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `work.reframe.tag` | Tag | Hard tech · Robotics |
| `work.reframe.title` | Title | Reframe Systems |
| `work.reframe.body` | Body | Reframe Systems builds robotics and custom software for prefab construction - strong technology in a slow, entrenched industry. The gap was storytelling, not technology: detailed explanations kept crowding out a simple, direct statement of what the company delivered. I coached one of the cofounders (Aaron) through an unscripted on-camera interview so he could separate the "what" from the "how" in his own words. The recording was used for recruiting, partner introductions, and as a general GTM asset. |
| `work.reframe.video` | Embed URL | https://www.youtube.com/embed/FPPJ4673UKQ |
| `work.reframe.pill.01` | Pill | Founder narrative |
| `work.reframe.pill.02` | Pill | Video |

### Additional experience

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `work.additional.heading` | Eyebrow | Additional experience |
| `work.additional.intro` | Body | Earlier work across categories that still informs how I work with hardware founders. |

| LOCATION | Tag | Body | Pills |
|----------|-----|------|-------|
| `work.additional.01` | Pre-IPO investor roadshow | Investor roadshow video series for a private tech company preparing to file an S-1 - narrative strategy through delivery with Opalite Media. The same discipline applies when a hardware company needs a crisp story for public markets, not just the next round. | Investor strategy · Video production |
| `work.additional.02` | Connected-care hardware (via Altitude) | User research and investment roadmapping for a global medical device manufacturer's physical fall-alert and aging-in-place line. In-home interviews, competitive audits, and adoption due diligence - the kind of work you do when a product has to earn trust in the real world, not just pass a demo. | User research · M&A roadmapping |
| `work.additional.03` | Physical product research (via Altitude) | Usability research with industrial designers and mechanical engineers for a major appliance OEM - in-kitchen food prep, cutting workflows, and tool performance. Ground-truth behavior research that informs what to build, similar to how hardware founders need to validate use cases before scaling GTM. | Usability testing · Market research |

## Section 5 - Close / CTA

| LOCATION | Type | Copy |
|----------|------|------|
| `contact.body.01` | Body | If your technology is genuine but the commercial story is still being figured out - that gap is solvable. Closing it earlier is usually worth more than closing it later. |
| `contact.body.02` | Body | Working with scientists and engineers who have spent years - or careers - on a single hard problem is not a task I take lightly. My job is to make sure that work is understood, fully believed, and backed by the people who can help bring it into the world. |
| `contact.availability` | Body | Open to FT roles or advisory work where commercial strategy is part of the build. |
| `contact.closing` | Body | I'd love to hear what you're building. |
| `contact.location` | Body | Based in Boston. |
| `contact.cta` | Button | Book a call → |
| `contact.cta.url` | URL | https://calendly.com/jeremyfinch_meeting/ |
